name: Apache Answer
what: Open-source Q&A platform for knowledge sharing, support answers, and Stack Overflow-style interactions.
solves: Structured question/answer knowledge exchange with lighter scope than a full forum.
users: Support communities, technical communities, and teams that want Q&A more than discussion.
fit: Conditional. Better than Discourse only if Hylono wants a narrow, structured Q&A/help surface instead of a broader community forum.
runtime: Go/Node-style web platform (verify exact runtime components) with DB/cache/storage and plugin/support features.
components: Q&A app, admin UI, database, storage, and optional integrations/plugins.
deploy: Docker quick-start exists; production still needs DB, storage, auth decisions, and moderation processes.
deps: Database, storage, mail/auth, reverse proxy, and backup plan.
auth: Local auth plus possible external provider integrations; verify current options.
apis: Q&A platform APIs and admin/plugin surfaces.
security: Public question content, user accounts, moderation, and spam controls require attention.
setup_path: Use only if the product need is specifically Q&A/help, not general community.
env: DB settings, storage, mail, auth secrets, and base URL.
operations: Moderation, spam handling, upgrade cadence, and taxonomy/content curation matter.
failure_points: Choosing a Q&A platform when Hylono really needs docs or community discussions instead.
integration: Possible link from site support/help surface and SSO via ZITADEL if supported.
verify: Choose between Discourse and Apache Answer explicitly; do not run both casually.
gdpr: User account and content data require standard privacy handling.
overlap: Narrower alternative to Discourse; likely redundant if BookStack/Docusaurus plus Discourse already exist.
codex_read_first: Read the overlap map and product-surface decision notes first.
codex_best_path: Only implement if Hylono wants a support Q&A surface and not a forum.
codex_never_assume: Never assume Q&A, docs, and community forums are interchangeable.
